blocks.content: At Kick Robotics in Maryland, I worked as a Robotics Engineer Intern and validated a custom software package for SLAM and autonomous navigation. I improved navigation accuracy by 20 percent and integrated camera and LiDAR sensors for warehouse monitoring.

At BAJA SAEINDIA, I served as Virtual Dynamics Division Lead and trained more than 1000 engineering students on digital-twin BAJA buggy development. I introduced the Validation Event for 170 student teams and helped correlate virtual vehicle fidelity with physical vehicle performance through real-time simulation.

As a Virtual Dynamics Division Member at BAJA SAEINDIA, I conducted IPG CarMaker training for over 800 engineering students, supported all-terrain BAJA buggy development, and served as a race commentator for a live audience of over 5000 during the endurance event.

At Resonance Racing, I served as Team Captain, led a 25-member team across technical, manufacturing, and marketing divisions, managed a budget above 25000 USD, directed red-team reviews and testing strategy, and improved productivity by 33 percent and cost-effectiveness by 15 percent.

As Powertrain Division Lead at Resonance Racing, I led development of a robust drivetrain unit for an ATV, engineered a custom 2-stage reduction gearbox, integrated verification through KISSsoft, applied SAE and BIS standards, and improved efficacy and failure-mode detection by 20 percent.

As a Powertrain Engineer at Resonance Racing, I performed strength and material-selection calculations for a custom gearbox and final-drive axles, streamlined the CAD process for vehicle assemblies, and achieved a 150 percent improvement in lead time.

As Vice Chairperson of the SAE Collegiate Club, I coordinated motorsports activities for six student teams, delivered presentations to administrators and stakeholders, and supported sponsorship negotiations and project funding.

At Terrapin Rockets, I simulated feature parameters to tune controller performance, developed multivariate Kalman filter library packages, integrated sensors into flight software, and engineered a custom controller for a sounding rocket air-brake system targeting an apogee of 10000 feet.
